_description: Ground-truth expected results from the PRAT TOSEM 2021 paper (doi:10.1145/3487568). Each entry specifies the exact paper-reported numbers and an acceptable tolerance range for CI validation.
_paper: Williams et al., Guided Feature Identification and Removal for Resource-constrained Firmware, ACM TOSEM 2021
_note: paper_lines is the value reported in Table 4. tolerance_pct allows for variation due to coverage tool version, test execution, platform, and absence of KLEE.

mosquitto-tls.notes: Paper used KLEE-enhanced coverage; without KLEE, expect lower end of range

ffmpeg-x264.notes: SCOPE SUBSTITUTION: x264's removable code lives in the EXTERNAL libx264 library, which FFmpeg only links; PRAT compiles only the in-tree wrapper (libavcodec/libx264.c, ~549 lines), so it cannot measure x264's real footprint. To exercise PRAT on an FFmpeg feature whose code is fully in-tree, the demo analyzes the self-contained DTS decoder (--disable-decoder=dca, 7 dedicated files). The reported number is for the dca decoder, NOT a reproduction of the paper's x264 value; it happens to fall in the same band.

uamqp-websockets.notes: azure-uamqp-c has been archived; version pinned to last stable

opendds-security.notes: OpenDDS Security is a large subsystem; version drift may affect counts

quiche-ffdhe.notes: CODEBASE DRIFT: 'ffdhe' is NOT a Cargo feature in quiche 0.20.1 (it is BoringSSL C config, not a Rust feature), so the paper's exact target cannot be built. The demo instead analyzes 'qlog' — a real, detectable feature with 63 #[cfg(feature="qlog")] sites across quiche/src, exercised by the crate's tests. The reported number is for qlog (interleaved, like the paper's interleaved features) and is NOT a reproduction of the paper's ffdhe value; its proximity to 450 is coincidental. Coverage uses stable cargo-llvm-cov (source-based) converted to gcov, since rustc's -Zprofile gcov path was removed.

aom-encoder.notes: AOM encoder is very large; exact count depends heavily on test coverage
